paper_title: Validation and Analysis of Pollen Typhae Methods

methods.description: Validation experiments were performed to investigate the precision, repeatability, and stability of the qualitative method using UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS for analyzing crude PT extract.

steps.description: The precision was assessed by one sample with six replicate injections.
steps.searchable_summary: assess precision using six replicate injections

steps.description: The repeatability of the method was investigated by using six replicate solutions.
steps.searchable_summary: investigate repeatability with six replicate solutions

steps.description: The stability of those analytes was assessed by analysing the solution at 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, and 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: assess stability by analyzing solution at multiple time points

methods.description: Validation of the quantitative method under optimum chromatographic conditions to ensure accuracy, precision, repeatability, and recovery for quantitative components.

steps.description: Under the optimum chromatographic conditions, the correlation coefficient values of 5 quantitative components were more than 0.9991.
steps.searchable_summary: validate correlation coefficients for quantitative components

steps.description: Repeatability was investigated by analysing 6 independent samples.
The intra-day and inter-days accuracies for 5 compounds at three levels (low, medium and high) were 86.1-112% and 86.7-112%, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: validate intra-day and inter-day accuracies at three levels

steps.description: Recovery was performed at low, medium and high levels (accurate adding 80, 100, and 120% of known amount of 5 mixed standards to the analyzed sample of PT).
steps.searchable_summary: perform recovery analysis at three levels

methods.description: Metabonomics analysis for screening combinatorial quality markers in natural products using UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS data processed by XCMS software.

steps.description: All chromatographic data of different batches of natural products obtained from UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS was processed by the XCMS software.
steps.searchable_summary: process chromatographic data using XCMS software

steps.description: P-values of T-tests were generated and the variables with P values less than 0.05 were chosen.
steps.searchable_summary: perform T-tests and select variables with P < 0.05

steps.description: Partial least squares discriminant analysis (PLS-DA) was employed to select markers with VIP > 1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PLS-DA to select markers with VIP > 1

methods.method_name: Quantitative Analysis of Different Batches of PT
methods.description: Quantitative analysis of combinatorial quality markers in different batches of PT using a validated UPLC-PDA method.

steps.description: The validated UPLC-PDA method was used to simultaneously determine the 5 combinatorial markers of 34 batches PT samples.

methods.description: Application of discriminant analysis to build a predictive model for group membership based on observed characteristics of quality markers.

steps.description: PLS-DA model was developed to validate grouping of PT samples based on markers.
steps.searchable_summary: develop PLS-DA model for sample grouping

steps.description: Discriminant function was applied to new samples for group assignment.
steps.searchable_summary: apply discriminant function for group assignment
